code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test02-MAR-2018Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal. These findings may indicate renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy; further nephrological evaluation and assessment for diabetes are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
